166 FUNDAMENTALS OF ASTROLOGY
(xxvi) If the lord of the Navamsa occupied by the lord of
IX house be in IV or V: or if Mercury be in conjunction with
or aspected by Jupiter; or if Mercury occup>ing a Kendra be
aspected by the lord of IX house, provided Jupiter is in the
ascendant.
(xxvii) If Saturn occupy in strength his Mulatrikona or
exaltation identical with a Kendra or Kona and be aspected by
the lord of XI house.
(xxviii) If the Moon be in I. Jupiter in IV. Venus in X, and
Saturn in his own Sign or exaltation; or if benelics be posited
in X XI, XII I, II. and III houses.
(xxix) If Saturn in strength be in Uttamamsa, Jupiter in a
Navamsa other than his debilitation, and the Sun in a benefic
Arhsa be aspected by benefics.
(xxx) If Rahu be in X, and Saturn occupying XI be aspected
by the lord of IX, and if the lord of the ascendant be unassociated
with a planet in debilitation.
(xxxi) Lords of IX and X counted from the Lagna —
Do Do .
